scope: Verify round-1 s1/s2/s3 disposition and scan commit 05c9732 for new security regressions introduced by the round-1 fixes (commitDraft isGenerating flip, observePersistedForHandover removal, runInferencePersistent USER-save ordering).

s1.note: Raw inference onError message logging unchanged in commit 05c9732 (lines 713-714 persistent, 800-ish quick). Confirmed out of Task-8 scope — Phase 2.5 logexport workstream concern. Leaving as deferred.

s2.note:
Both `savedOk` guards verified:
  - USER path line 628-633: savePersistentMessage return routed through isSuccess → gates updateChatLastMessage.
  - ASSISTANT path line 697-702: same pattern on done=true branch.
The timestamp-without-row data-integrity gap from round-1 s2 is closed on both paths. However, the original recommendation also asked to abort the inference dispatch on USER-persist failure (`return@launch` + snackbar). The current fix gates only `updateChatLastMessage`; `dispatchInferencePersistent(...)` at line 635 still fires unconditionally when the USER save failed. See new finding s4 below — this is a residual, lower-severity slice of the same concern and is filed as minor rather than blocking.

s3.note: Nested errorLog.launch drops on scope cancellation — unchanged, explicitly out-of-scope per round-1 disposition (architectural, requires ApplicationScope injection).

commitDraft_isGenerating_flip_M1.analysis: Line 548 flips `_uiState.value = ChatUiState.Ready(isGenerating = true)` synchronously on the main thread before the `viewModelScope.launch` at line 556, closing a rapid double-tap TOCTOU on the Send gate. The onFailure branch (line 577) restores `isGenerating = false`. One minor UX caveat (not security): if `observeEngineState` emits a status update while the commit is in-flight, `updateUiStateFromEntry` may recompute uiState from engine status and erase the isGenerating flip — but this is a race on UI state, not on persistence or authorization, and no double-write or double-commit is possible because `chatRepository.commitDraftChat` is itself the atomicity boundary. No new A01/A04/A08 exposure.

observePersistedForHandover_removal_m2.analysis: Deleting the side-effect collector (old lines 514-524) leaves the combine in `buildMessagesFlow` as the single invariant. Round-1 T1 strengthened the test to "ASSISTANT count ≤ 1 in every emission" independent of the streaming flag, so the load-bearing invariant is now enforced by the test suite. No data-integrity or access-control surface; the removed function wrote only to in-memory `_streamingMessage`. No logging was lost (it was a no-log side-effect). Clean.

runInferencePersistent_ordering_change.analysis: Save ordering is: launch → savePersistentMessage(USER) → gated updateChatLastMessage → unconditional dispatchInferencePersistent. The fix correctly addresses the drawer-timestamp lie from round-1 s2, but allows runInference to stream a response to a USER message that does not exist in Room. Filed as s4 minor below.

mapNotNull_m4.analysis: `registry.activeModelName.mapNotNull { it }.first()` (line 475) is semantically equivalent to the old `first { it != null }!!` — suspends until non-null, then returns the value. No change in trust boundary or failure mode.

reinitInProgress_m3.analysis: Dropping the redundant `_reinitInProgress = false` assignments inside `try` is safe — the `finally` block already clears the flag on every return path. No lock-stuck scenario introduced.

new_findings.title: dispatchInferencePersistent runs unconditionally after a failed USER savePersistentMessage
new_findings.description: The round-1 s2 fix gates `updateChatLastMessage` on `savedOk`, but `dispatchInferencePersistent(identity, text, pending, model, accumulateThinking)` at line 635 executes regardless of whether the USER row landed in Room. When `savePersistentMessage(userMsg)` throws (SQLiteFullException, SQLiteConstraintException, or any IO failure on `Dispatchers.IO`), the VM proceeds to stream tokens from the engine and — on done=true — persists an ASSISTANT row at line 697 referencing a `chatId` whose new USER turn is absent. Violates the spirit of AC-R1 (USER persisted BEFORE runInference fires): the guarantee is now "USER persist was attempted before runInference," not "USER persist succeeded."
new_findings.impact: Data-integrity gap rather than an exploit surface: on USER-save failure the persisted history shows an orphan ASSISTANT bubble with no preceding USER prompt, while the in-memory `_streamingMessage` still shows the USER bubble (appended by the repository-side path? — actually the VM does not append USER in-memory for Persistent mode; the combine reads USER from Room). A process kill between the failed USER INSERT and the successful ASSISTANT INSERT produces a persisted ASSISTANT with no USER — the inverse of AC-R3's stated invariant ("kill mid-stream leaves USER+no-ASSISTANT"). The attacker surface is null (no cross-user or privilege boundary), but the post-mortem log is the only signal the user has that anything went wrong, which intersects with deferred s3.
new_findings.recommendation:
Fold the early-abort into the existing launch as round-1 s2 originally recommended:
```kotlin
val savedOk = runCatching { chatRepository.savePersistentMessage(userMsg) }
    .onFailure { errorLog.e("history-write", "savePersistentMessage USER failed", it) }
    .isSuccess
if (!savedOk) {
    _snackbar.tryEmit(R.string.chat_load_failed_title)
    _streamingMessage.value = null
    _uiState.value = ChatUiState.Ready(isGenerating = false)
    return@launch
}
chatRepository.updateChatLastMessage(identity.id, userTs)
dispatchInferencePersistent(identity, text, pending, model, accumulateThinking)
```
This restores the full AC-R1 invariant (USER row is present in Room before any engine work begins) and avoids ever persisting an orphan ASSISTANT. Does not block merge — the failure mode requires disk-full / Room constraint violation, and the ASSISTANT-orphan window is bounded by inference duration.

verdict_rationale: Round-1 s2 is closed on the original exploit scenario (timestamp lie in drawer). s1 and s3 remain deferred per round-1 disposition. No OWASP A01-A10 regression introduced by the M1/m2/m3/m4 changes — commitDraft gate flip is a pure in-memory state flip with no auth or persistence implications; observePersistedForHandover removal is covered by strengthened combine invariant tests; mapNotNull refactor is semantically identical. One new minor finding (s4) extends the s2 fix: dispatchInferencePersistent should also be gated on USER savedOk. Zero critical or major findings — approved for merge; s4 can be addressed in follow-up or as a trailing round-2 touch-up if desired.
